9 min read
Just now

--

Every OCR model parses a page beautifully and then forgets the page before it. Tables split across page breaks, headings flatten, captions detach from figures. MinerU-Popo is a small post-processing model that rebuilds the document’s structure after parsing, and it lifts downstream RAG accuracy as a result.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Knowledge transformation in data flowEarlier in this series we looked at MinerU and Docling, two of the best open-source document parsers available currently.
They read a PDF page and extract its elements with real accuracy including paragraphs, tables, headings, figures, each with bounding boxes and text. For a single page, modern OCR is largely solved.
The problem is that documents are not single pages and parsers work one page at a time. A table starts at the bottom of page four and continues at the top of page five. The parser sees two separate tables.
A paragraph breaks across a column or around a figure. The parser sees two unrelated text blocks.
If you hit the paywall, use the friend link here: read the full post.
A section heading on one page governs content three pages later. The parser has no idea they are related. A figure on page seven is referenced by a caption on page eight. The parser never links them.
